title: Unicode text and formatting deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Unicode text and formatting; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Unicode text and formatting: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? One Python character is always one displayed glyph and calling lower makes arbitrary Unicode text safely comparable.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Treat str as Unicode code points, define normalization and case policy for the domain, and encode or decode explicitly only at byte boundaries.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Treat str as Unicode code points, define normalization and case policy for the domain, and encode or decode explicitly only at byte boundaries.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Treat str as Unicode code points, define normalization and case policy for the domain, and encode or decode explicitly only at byte boundaries.
One Python character is always one displayed glyph and calling lower makes arbitrary Unicode text safely comparable.

answer.explanation: Treat str as Unicode code points, define normalization and case policy for the domain, and encode or decode explicitly only at byte boundaries.

items.title: Unicode text and formatting: canonical checkpoint
items.prompt: Complete the source-aligned Python: Beginner to Intermediate correction with the exact canonical phrase used by this lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'byte boundaries'. Treat str as Unicode code points, define normalization and case policy for the domain, and encode or decode explicitly only at byte boundaries.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Treat str as Unicode code points, define normalization and case policy for the domain, and encode or decode explicitly only at byte boundaries.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

items.title: Unicode text and formatting: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read one line of Unicode text. Normalize runs of whitespace with split/join, then print the normalized text and its code-point length.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Python: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: Python str operations treat the input as Unicode text, and encoding is intentionally absent from this in-memory boundary.
